terminal.c(3) Library Functions Manual terminal.c(3)

NAME

terminal.c - Pixel renderer for render-file: libvterm + FreeType2 software compositor.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct terminal_renderer_s

Functions


asciichat_error_t term_renderer_create (const term_renderer_config_t *cfg, terminal_renderer_t **out)
asciichat_error_t term_renderer_feed (terminal_renderer_t *r, const char *ansi_frame, size_t len)
const uint8_t * term_renderer_pixels (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_width_px (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_height_px (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_pitch (terminal_renderer_t *r)
void term_renderer_destroy (terminal_renderer_t *r)

Detailed Description

Pixel renderer for render-file: libvterm + FreeType2 software compositor.

Cross-platform implementation using:

  • libvterm: Terminal emulation without any display backend
  • FreeType2: Glyph rasterization
  • fontconfig: Font resolution (Linux/macOS)

No platform-specific code (#if guards) needed — this code compiles on all platforms.

Definition in file media/render/terminal.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t term_renderer_create (const term_renderer_config_t * cfg, terminal_renderer_t ** out)

Definition at line 104 of file media/render/terminal.c.

104                                                                                                      {
105   terminal_renderer_t *r = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*r), terminal_renderer_t *);
106   r->cols = cfg->cols;
107   r->rows = cfg->rows;
108   r->theme = cfg->theme;
109 
110   log_debug_every(1000, "term_renderer_create: Initializing FreeType");
111   if (FT_Init_FreeType(&r->ft_lib)) {
112     SAFE_FREE(r);
113     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "FreeType init failed");
114   }
115 
116   // Load font from either file path or memory
117   if (cfg->font_data && cfg->font_data_size > 0) {
118     log_debug_every(1000, "term_renderer_create: Loading font from memory (%zu bytes)", cfg->font_data_size);
119     if (FT_New_Memory_Face(r->ft_lib, cfg->font_data, (FT_Long)cfg->font_data_size, 0, &r->ft_face)) {
120       FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
121       SAFE_FREE(r);
122       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "FreeType: cannot load bundled font");
123     }
124   } else {
125     log_debug_every(1000, "term_renderer_create: Loading font from path '%s'", cfg->font_spec);
126     if (FT_New_Face(r->ft_lib, cfg->font_spec, 0, &r->ft_face)) {
127       FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
128       SAFE_FREE(r);
129       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "FreeType: cannot load font '%s'", cfg->font_spec);
130     }
131   }
132 
133   // Detect if this is the matrix font (needs character mapping to Private Use Area)
134   r->is_matrix_font = (strstr(cfg->font_spec, "matrix") != NULL || strstr(cfg->font_spec, "Matrix") != NULL);
135   if (r->is_matrix_font) {
136     log_debug("term_renderer_create: Detected matrix font - will use Private Use Area character mapping");
137   }
138 
139   // Handle scalable vs bitmap fonts differently
140   // Bitmap fonts (like matrix) don't respond to FT_Set_Char_Size
141   // Instead, we need to select the best available bitmap strike
142   log_debug("term_renderer_create: font='%s' num_fixed_sizes=%d", cfg->font_spec, r->ft_face->num_fixed_sizes);
143   if (r->ft_face->num_fixed_sizes > 0) {
144     // Bitmap font: select the best matching bitmap strike
145     int err = FT_Select_Size(r->ft_face, 0); // Use first available strike
146     log_debug("term_renderer_create: [BITMAP] FT_Select_Size(0) returned %d", err);
147   } else {
148     // Scalable font: use FT_Set_Char_Size
149     // FT_Set_Char_Size takes 1/64pt units and DPI — supports fractional point sizes.
150     // 96 DPI is the standard screen DPI used here; the 64 factor is the 26.6 fixed-point scale.
151     int err = FT_Set_Char_Size(r->ft_face, 0, (FT_F26Dot6)(cfg->font_size_pt * 64.0), 96, 96);
152     log_debug("term_renderer_create: [SCALABLE] FT_Set_Char_Size(size_pt=%.1f → %ld 1/64pt) returned %d",
153               cfg->font_size_pt, (long)(cfg->font_size_pt * 64.0), err);
154   }
155 
156   int load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'M', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
157   log_debug("DEBUG: FT_Load_Char('M', FT_LOAD_RENDER) returned %d", load_err);
158   log_debug("DEBUG: glyph->bitmap.rows=%d, glyph->bitmap.width=%d, glyph->bitmap_top=%d",
159             r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap.rows, r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap.width, r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap_top);
160 
161   // For monospace ASCII grid: use advance.x (proper character spacing) and rendered height
162   FT_Pos advance_x_26_6 = r->ft_face->glyph->advance.x;
163   log_debug("DEBUG: advance.x (26.6pt)=%ld", advance_x_26_6);
164 
165   // Calculate cell width from font metrics
166   r->cell_w = (int)(advance_x_26_6 >> 6);
167 
168   // Only add spacing to fill entire frame if --stretch is specified
169   bool stretch_mode = GET_OPTION(stretch);
170   if (stretch_mode) {
171     r->cell_w++;
172     log_debug("ADVANCE_X: value=%ld (26.6pt) → cell_w=%d (advance + 1 for stretch), cols=%d → width_px=%d",
173               advance_x_26_6, r->cell_w, r->cols, r->cols * r->cell_w);
174   } else {
175     log_debug("ADVANCE_X: value=%ld (26.6pt) → cell_w=%d (preserve aspect ratio), cols=%d → width_px=%d",
176               advance_x_26_6, r->cell_w, r->cols, r->cols * r->cell_w);
177   }
178 
179   // For both bitmap and scalable fonts, use the actual glyph bitmap height
180   // This ensures text doesn't overflow cells. Using size->metrics.height (line spacing)
181   // instead of bitmap.rows causes cells to be too large and text to overflow.
182   r->cell_h = r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap.rows;
183   int raw_bitmap_top = r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap_top;
184   log_debug("DEBUG: cell_h=%d (from bitmap.rows), raw_bitmap_top=%d", r->cell_h, raw_bitmap_top);
185 
186   // Apply aspect ratio correction unless --stretch is specified
187   // Terminal characters are typically 2:1 (height:width) to appear normal
188   if (!stretch_mode) {
189     int corrected_h = r->cell_w * 2;
190     if (corrected_h != r->cell_h && r->cell_h > 0) {
191       log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Correcting cell_h from %d to %d (2x width=%d)", r->cell_h, corrected_h, r->cell_w);
192       r->cell_h = corrected_h;
193     }
194   }
195 
196   // Position baseline vertically within the cell.
197   // Baseline should be positioned at approximately 75% of cell height to allow proper
198   // glyph rendering with space both above and below. This prevents glyphs from being
199   // clipped at the cell boundaries.
200   //
201   // Baseline position: y_glyph = py + r->baseline - g->bitmap_top
202   // We want: 0 <= baseline - bitmap_top < cell_h (glyph fits within cell)
203   // Choose: baseline = (3 * cell_h) / 4 (positions at 75% height)
204   r->baseline = (3 * r->cell_h) / 4;
205   log_debug("DEBUG: baseline set to %d (75%% of cell_h=%d)", r->baseline, r->cell_h);
206 
207   r->width_px = r->cols * r->cell_w;
208   r->height_px = r->rows * r->cell_h;
209   log_debug("DEBUG: calculated dimensions: width_px=%d (cols=%d * cell_w=%d), height_px=%d (rows=%d * cell_h=%d)",
210             r->width_px, r->cols, r->cell_w, r->height_px, r->rows, r->cell_h);
211   // Pitch in bytes: align to 4-byte boundary for proper row alignment
212   // Each pixel is 3 bytes (RGB), so we calculate base pitch and round up
213   int base_pitch = r->width_px * 3;
214   r->pitch = (base_pitch + 3) & ~3; // Round up to next multiple of 4
215   log_debug("PITCH_CALC: width_px=%d, base_pitch=%d, final_pitch=%d (padded by %d bytes)", r->width_px, base_pitch,
216             r->pitch, r->pitch - base_pitch);
217   log_debug("DEBUG: Allocating framebuffer: %d bytes total (%d pitch * %d height)",
218             (int)((size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px), r->pitch, r->height_px);
219   r->framebuffer = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px, uint8_t *);
220 
221   r->vt = vterm_new(r->rows, r->cols);
222   r->vts = vterm_obtain_screen(r->vt);
223 
224   // Set size BEFORE reset to ensure screen is allocated with correct dimensions
225   vterm_set_size(r->vt, r->rows, r->cols);
226   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_set_size called with rows=%d cols=%d", r->rows, r->cols);
227 
228   vterm_screen_set_callbacks(r->vts, &g_vterm_cbs, r);
229   // Reset MUST come before any input writing to initialize state correctly
230   vterm_screen_reset(r->vts, 1);
231 
232   // Verify the size was set correctly after reset
233   int vt_rows, vt_cols;
234   vterm_get_size(r->vt, &vt_rows, &vt_cols);
235   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_get_size returned rows=%d cols=%d (expected %d,%d)", vt_rows, vt_cols, r->rows, r->cols);
236 
237   // CRITICAL: Set size AGAIN after reset, as reset may have affected it
238   vterm_set_size(r->vt, r->rows, r->cols);
239   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_set_size called AGAIN after reset");
240 
241   *out = r;
242   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
243 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    terminal_renderer_s::baseline, terminal_renderer_s::cell_h,
    terminal_renderer_s::cell_w, term_renderer_config_t::cols,

    term_renderer_config_t::font_data,

    terminal_renderer_s::is_matrix_font, log_debug,
    log_debug_every, terminal_renderer_s::pitch,
    term_renderer_config_t::rows, terminal_renderer_s::rows,

    term_renderer_config_t::theme, terminal_renderer_s::theme,
    terminal_renderer_s::vt, terminal_renderer_s::vts, and
    terminal_renderer_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_create().






void
  term_renderer_destroy (terminal_renderer_t * r)


Definition at line 520 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


520                                                    {
521   if (!r)
522     return;
523   vterm_free(r->vt);
524   FT_Done_Face(r->ft_face);
525   FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
526   SAFE_FREE(r->framebuffer);
527   SAFE_FREE(r);
528 }






References terminal_renderer_s::framebuffer,

    SAFE_FREE, and terminal_renderer_s::vt.

asciichat_error_t term_renderer_feed
  (terminal_renderer_t * r, const char * ansi_frame, size_t len)


Definition at line 245 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


245                                                                                                  {
246   // Clear framebuffer to ensure no leftover pixels from previous frames
247   uint8_t def_bg = (r->theme == TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT) ? 255 : 0;
248   memset(r->framebuffer, def_bg, (size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px);
249 
250   // DEBUG: Log cell dimensions and font info
251   static int feed_count = 0;
252   if (feed_count++ < 2) {
253     log_debug("TERM_RENDERER_FEED[%d]: Grid=%dx%d, Cell=(%d,%d)px, Output=(%dx%d)px, Baseline=%d", feed_count, r->cols,
254               r->rows, r->cell_w, r->cell_h, r->width_px, r->height_px, r->baseline);
255   }
256 
257   static const char home[] = "\033[H";
258   vterm_input_write(r->vt, home, sizeof(home) - 1);
259 
260   // Count newlines to determine CRLF conversion size
261   int newline_count = 0;
262   for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
263     if (ansi_frame[i] == '\n')
264       newline_count++;
265   }
266 
267   // Convert LF-only line endings to CRLF (vterm expects CRLF)
268   // This fixes cursor positioning on alternate rows
269   char *fixed_frame = SAFE_MALLOC(len + newline_count, char *);
270   if (!fixed_frame) {
271     log_error("failed to allocate memory for CRLF conversion");
272     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "CRLF conversion allocation failed");
273   }
274 
275   size_t fixed_pos = 0;
276   for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
277     fixed_frame[fixed_pos++] = ansi_frame[i];
278     if (ansi_frame[i] == '\n' && (i == 0 || ansi_frame[i - 1] != '\r')) {
279       // Insert carriage return before LF if not already preceded by CR
280       fixed_frame[fixed_pos - 1] = '\r';
281       fixed_frame[fixed_pos++] = '\n';
282     }
283   }
284 
285   vterm_input_write(r->vt, fixed_frame, fixed_pos);
286   log_debug("DEBUG: fed ANSI frame to vterm with CRLF line endings (original_len=%zu, fixed_len=%zu)", len, fixed_pos);
287   SAFE_FREE(fixed_frame);
288 
289   // Check ALL line lengths to see if there's an alternating pattern
290   size_t line_num = 0;
291   size_t pos = 0;
292   size_t visible_chars = 0;
293   bool in_ansi = false;
294 
295   while (pos < len && line_num < 45) { // Check all 42 lines
296     char c = ansi_frame[pos];
297 
298     if (c == '\033') {
299       in_ansi = true;
300     } else if (in_ansi && c == 'm') {
301       in_ansi = false;
302     } else if (!in_ansi && c == '\n') {
303       if (line_num < 10 || line_num >= 40) {
304         log_debug("DEBUG: ANSI line %zu has %zu visible chars", line_num, visible_chars);
305       }
306       visible_chars = 0;
307       line_num++;
308     } else if (!in_ansi) {
309       visible_chars++;
310     }
311     pos++;
312   }
313 
314   int cells_with_chars = 0, cells_rendered = 0;
315   log_debug("DEBUG: RENDER_LOOP starting - grid is %d rows × %d cols, cell dimensions w=%d h=%d", r->rows, r->cols,
316             r->cell_w, r->cell_h);
317 
318   for (int row = 0; row < r->rows; row++) {
319     for (int col = 0; col < r->cols; col++) {
320       VTermScreenCell cell;
321       vterm_screen_get_cell(r->vts, (VTermPos){row, col}, &cell);
322 
323       // Debug sample: first row, every 50th column
324       if (row == 0 && col % 50 == 0) {
325         log_debug("DEBUG: ROW0 COL%d: char=0x%02x ('%c'), has_fg=%d has_bg=%d", col, cell.chars[0],
326                   (cell.chars[0] >= 32 && cell.chars[0] < 127) ? cell.chars[0] : '?', VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.fg),
327                   VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.bg));
328       }
329 
330       uint8_t fr, fg, fb, br, bg, bb;
331       if (VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.fg)) {
332         fr = cell.fg.rgb.red;
333         fg = cell.fg.rgb.green;
334         fb = cell.fg.rgb.blue;
335       } else {
336         fr = fg = fb = 204;
337       }
338 
339       if (VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.bg)) {
340         br = cell.bg.rgb.red;
341         bg = cell.bg.rgb.green;
342         bb = cell.bg.rgb.blue;
343       } else {
344         br = bg = bb = def_bg;
345       }
346 
347       // DEBUG: Log color extraction for sample cells
348       if ((row == 0 && col % 50 == 0) || (row == 5 && col % 50 == 0)) {
349         log_debug("COLORS_EXTRACTED: row=%d col=%d char=0x%02x FG=RGB(%u,%u,%u) BG=RGB(%u,%u,%u)", row, col,
350                   cell.chars[0], fr, fg, fb, br, bg, bb);
351       }
352 
353       int px = col * r->cell_w, py = row * r->cell_h;
354       for (int dy = 0; dy < r->cell_h; dy++) {
355         int y = py + dy;
356         if (y < 0 || y >= r->height_px)
357           continue;
358         uint8_t *line = r->framebuffer + y * r->pitch + px * 3;
359         for (int dx = 0; dx < r->cell_w; dx++) {
360           int x = px + dx;
361           if (x < 0 || x >= r->width_px)
362             continue;
363           line[dx * 3] = br;
364           line[dx * 3 + 1] = bg;
365           line[dx * 3 + 2] = bb;
366         }
367       }
368 
369       // Debug: log bounds for rightmost column
370       if (col == r->cols - 1 && row == 0) {
371         log_debug("DEBUG: CELL_BG_FILL: col=%d px=%d cell_w=%d extends to px=%d (width_px=%d) cell would extend to "
372                   "pixel %d%s",
373                   col, px, r->cell_w, px + r->cell_w - 1, r->width_px, px + r->cell_w,
374                   (px + r->cell_w > r->width_px) ? " [OVERFLOW!]" : "");
375       }
376 
377       // Debug: log all characters in rightmost column
378       if (col == r->cols - 1) {
379         int char_code = (int)(unsigned char)cell.chars[0];
380         char char_display = (char_code >= 32 && char_code < 127) ? (char)char_code : '?';
381         log_debug("DEBUG: RIGHTMOST_CHAR: row=%d col=%d char=0x%02x (dec %d '%c') space=%d", row, col,
382                   (unsigned char)cell.chars[0], char_code, char_display, (cell.chars[0] == ' ' ? 1 : 0));
383       }
384 
385       if (cell.chars[0] && cell.chars[0] != ' ') {
386         cells_with_chars++;
387         // For matrix font, map ASCII characters to Private Use Area glyphs (U+E900-U+E91A)
388         uint32_t char_to_render = r->is_matrix_font ? matrix_char_map(cell.chars[0]) : cell.chars[0];
389         FT_UInt gi = FT_Get_Char_Index(r->ft_face, char_to_render);
390 
391         // Debug: sample cells (every 50 cols, every 5 rows) AND all cells in rightmost column
392         int is_rightmost = (col == r->cols - 1);
393         int is_sample = (col % 50 == 0 && row % 5 == 0);
394         if (is_sample || is_rightmost) {
395           log_debug("DEBUG: GLYPH_LOOKUP: row=%d col=%d char=0x%02x ('%c') → gi=%u%s", row, col, cell.chars[0],
396                     (cell.chars[0] >= 32 && cell.chars[0] < 127) ? cell.chars[0] : '?', gi,
397                     is_rightmost ? " [RIGHTMOST]" : "");
398         }
399 
400         if (gi) {
401           if (FT_Load_Glyph(r->ft_face, gi, FT_LOAD_RENDER) == 0) {
402             FT_GlyphSlot g = r->ft_face->glyph;
403 
404             // Blit if we have content
405             if (g->bitmap.width > 0 && g->bitmap.rows > 0) {
406               // Debug: sample rendered glyphs AND all rightmost column glyphs
407               if (is_sample || is_rightmost) {
408                 log_debug("DEBUG: GLYPH_RENDER: row=%d col=%d bitmap(%dx%d) → px=%d py=%d blit_pos(x=%d,y=%d) "
409                           "offset(bmp_left=%d, baseline=%d, bmp_top=%d)%s",
410                           row, col, g->bitmap.width, g->bitmap.rows, px, py, px + g->bitmap_left,
411                           py + r->baseline - g->bitmap_top, g->bitmap_left, r->baseline, g->bitmap_top,
412                           is_rightmost ? " [RIGHTMOST]" : "");
413               }
414               blit_glyph(r, &g->bitmap, px + g->bitmap_left, py + r->baseline - g->bitmap_top, fr, fg, fb, br, bg, bb);
415               cells_rendered++;
416             } else if (is_sample || is_rightmost) {
417               log_debug("DEBUG: GLYPH_EMPTY: row=%d col=%d gi=%u has empty bitmap (%dx%d)%s", row, col, gi,
418                         g->bitmap.width, g->bitmap.rows, is_rightmost ? " [RIGHTMOST]" : "");
419             }
420           } else if (is_sample || is_rightmost) {
421             log_debug("DEBUG: GLYPH_LOAD_FAIL: row=%d col=%d gi=%u FT_Load_Glyph failed%s", row, col, gi,
422                       is_rightmost ? " [RIGHTMOST]" : "");
423           }
424         } else if (is_sample || is_rightmost) {
425           log_debug("DEBUG: GLYPH_NOT_FOUND: row=%d col=%d char=0x%02x no glyph index%s", row, col, cell.chars[0],
426                     is_rightmost ? " [RIGHTMOST]" : "");
427         }
428       }
429     }
430   }
431 
432   log_debug("DEBUG: render loop finished - processed %d cells total", r->rows * r->cols);
433 
434   // Sample pixels from framebuffer to verify content
435   uint8_t *sample_top = r->framebuffer;
436   uint8_t *sample_mid = r->framebuffer + (r->height_px / 2) * r->pitch;
437   uint8_t *sample_bot = r->framebuffer + (r->height_px - 1) * r->pitch;
438 
439   // Enhanced pixel sampling
440   log_debug("DEBUG: pixel samples:");
441   log_debug("  top_left RGB(%d,%d,%d), top_mid RGB(%d,%d,%d), top_right RGB(%d,%d,%d)", sample_top[0], sample_top[1],
442             sample_top[2], sample_top[r->pitch / 2], sample_top[r->pitch / 2 + 1], sample_top[r->pitch / 2 + 2],
443             sample_top[(r->width_px - 1) * 3], sample_top[(r->width_px - 1) * 3 + 1],
444             sample_top[(r->width_px - 1) * 3 + 2]);
445   log_debug("  mid_left RGB(%d,%d,%d), mid_mid RGB(%d,%d,%d), mid_right RGB(%d,%d,%d)", sample_mid[0], sample_mid[1],
446             sample_mid[2], sample_mid[r->pitch / 2], sample_mid[r->pitch / 2 + 1], sample_mid[r->pitch / 2 + 2],
447             sample_mid[(r->width_px - 1) * 3], sample_mid[(r->width_px - 1) * 3 + 1],
448             sample_mid[(r->width_px - 1) * 3 + 2]);
449   log_debug("  bot_left RGB(%d,%d,%d), bot_mid RGB(%d,%d,%d), bot_right RGB(%d,%d,%d)", sample_bot[0], sample_bot[1],
450             sample_bot[2], sample_bot[r->pitch / 2], sample_bot[r->pitch / 2 + 1], sample_bot[r->pitch / 2 + 2],
451             sample_bot[(r->width_px - 1) * 3], sample_bot[(r->width_px - 1) * 3 + 1],
452             sample_bot[(r->width_px - 1) * 3 + 2]);
453 
454   log_debug("term_renderer_feed: cells_with_chars=%d, cells_rendered=%d", cells_with_chars, cells_rendered);
455 
456   // Write ANSI frame size and sample bottom row characters to debug file
457   FILE *dbg_dims = fopen("/tmp/render-dims.txt", "a");
458   if (dbg_dims) {
459     fprintf(dbg_dims, "[TERM_FEED] len=%zu, grid=%dx%d, pixels=%dx%d, cells_with_chars=%d, cells_rendered=%d\n", len,
460             r->cols, r->rows, r->width_px, r->height_px, cells_with_chars, cells_rendered);
461 
462     // Sample bottom row to see what characters are there
463     fprintf(dbg_dims, "  Bottom row (41): ");
464     for (int col = 0; col < 20; col++) { // First 20 chars
465       VTermScreenCell cell;
466       vterm_screen_get_cell(r->vts, (VTermPos){r->rows - 1, col}, &cell);
467       if (cell.chars[0]) {
468         fprintf(dbg_dims, "%c", (cell.chars[0] >= 32 && cell.chars[0] < 127) ? cell.chars[0] : '?');
469       } else {
470         fprintf(dbg_dims, " ");
471       }
472     }
473     fprintf(dbg_dims, "\n");
474 
475     // Check color of first char in bottom row
476     for (int col = 0; col < r->cols; col++) {
477       VTermScreenCell cell;
478       vterm_screen_get_cell(r->vts, (VTermPos){r->rows - 1, col}, &cell);
479       if (cell.chars[0] && cell.chars[0] != ' ') {
480         int has_rgb_fg = VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.fg);
481         int has_rgb_bg = VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.bg);
482         fprintf(dbg_dims, "  Colors - has_rgb_fg=%d has_rgb_bg=%d\n", has_rgb_fg, has_rgb_bg);
483         if (has_rgb_fg) {
484           fprintf(dbg_dims, "    fg=RGB(%d,%d,%d)\n", cell.fg.rgb.red, cell.fg.rgb.green, cell.fg.rgb.blue);
485         }
486         if (has_rgb_bg) {
487           fprintf(dbg_dims, "    bg=RGB(%d,%d,%d)\n", cell.bg.rgb.red, cell.bg.rgb.green, cell.bg.rgb.blue);
488         }
489         break;
490       }
491     }
492     fflush(dbg_dims);
493     fclose(dbg_dims);
494   }
495 
496   log_debug("term_renderer_feed: Grid dimensions: %d cols x %d rows, Pixel dimensions: %d x %d px", r->cols, r->rows,
497             r->width_px, r->height_px);
498 
499   log_debug(
500       "term_renderer_feed: pixel samples - top_left RGB(%d,%d,%d), mid_left RGB(%d,%d,%d), bot_left RGB(%d,%d,%d)",
501       sample_top[0], sample_top[1], sample_top[2], sample_mid[0], sample_mid[1], sample_mid[2], sample_bot[0],
502       sample_bot[1], sample_bot[2]);
503 
504   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
505 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    terminal_renderer_s::baseline, terminal_renderer_s::cell_h,
    terminal_renderer_s::cell_w, terminal_renderer_s::cols,
    ERROR_MEMORY, terminal_renderer_s::framebuffer,
    terminal_renderer_s::ft_face, terminal_renderer_s::height_px,
    terminal_renderer_s::is_matrix_font, log_debug,
    log_error, terminal_renderer_s::pitch,
    terminal_renderer_s::rows, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT,
    terminal_renderer_s::theme, terminal_renderer_s::vt,
    terminal_renderer_s::vts, and
  terminal_renderer_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_write_frame().
